data.title: 28 BLACK Energy Drink introduced at Anuga Fair in India, aiming to showcase unique brand lifestyle.
data.description: In Mumbai, India, the energy drink company 28 BLACK showcased its products at Anuga Select India 2025 to introduce its unique brand ethos. The brand prides itself on the concept that each day has "28 hours" of energy, emphasizing a distinctive lifestyle of embracing individuality. The focus was on designing a booth that departed from typical setups, opting out of aggressive lighting, information overload, and excessive promotional materials. Instead, the brand aimed to reflect its core values of energy and uniqueness within the booth's ambiance.

data.title: Travel Answers Group appoints Jonna Jackson as Director - Europe, driving strategic expansion in Europe.
data.description: In Bellevue, Washington on September 18, 2025, Travel Answers Group revealed Jonna Jackson as the new Director for Europe. Travel Answers is a prominent provider of unique vacation experiences. This appointment signifies a notable achievement as Travel Answers introduces its carefully crafted Europe line-up, showcasing the company's dedication to delivering exceptional travel experiences.

data.title: Tesla and Samsung's $16.5 billion chip deal at Samsung's Texas fab strengthens both companies' ambitions in the chip industry.
data.description: In Taipei on September 18, 2025, it was reported that Tesla and Samsung reached a significant agreement worth US$16.5 billion for the production of Tesla's upcoming AI6 chips at Samsung's Texas fabrication plant. The deal is perceived as mutually beneficial, with Tesla benefiting from tailored supply chain assistance and Samsung enhancing its reputation in the foundry sector.

data.title: Dow Jones futures surge as stock market hits record highs, driven by Nvidia deal and Fed reaction. Palantir leads 7 new buys.
data.description: Major stock market indexes surged to new record levels driven by a significant deal involving Nvidia and a response from the Federal Reserve on the second day. Among the top performers were Palantir and Spotify, leading new purchases. Dow Jones Futures experienced an increase as the stock market raced to highs, with Palantir leading the charge with 7 new buys. Investors are showing strong interest in these companies, reflecting a bullish sentiment in the market.

data.title: Grvt secures $19M funding to boost privacy-focused onchain finance, a breakthrough for trillion-dollar markets.
data.description: Today, in Panama City, the leading DEX (decentralized exchange) for onchain financial privacy, Grvt, utilizing zero-knowledge ("ZK") technology, has finalized a Series A funding round, securing $19 million. This significant investment further solidifies Grvt's innovative approach in the industry.

data.title: IASO Biotherapeutics released three-year follow-up data for CAR-T cell therapy Fucaso for multiple myeloma treatment in 2025.
data.description: On September 18, 2025, in Shanghai and Nanjing, China, along with Pleasanton, California, IASO Biotherapeutics disclosed significant news. The company, renowned for its work in creating, advancing, producing, and promoting cutting-edge cell therapies, shared details of a major development. This involves a 36-month project aimed at accelerating their initiatives and reaching new milestones.

data.title: HIKSEMI presents smart storage tech at 2025 China-Central Asia Expo in Uzbekistan, from Sept 16-18 at Tashkent, boasting innovation in data solutions.
data.description: A technology company, HIKSEMI, known for its cutting-edge data storage solutions, recently took part in the 2025 China-Central Asia (Uzbekistan) Trade Exhibition in Tashkent. The exhibition ran from September 16 to 18 at the Tashkent International Convention Center. HIKSEMI showcased its products and services at this global event, highlighting their innovative approach to data storage. This collaboration between China and Central Asia attracted significant attention, with attendees and participants numbering in the thousands.

data.title: Rex-Osprey's XRP and DOGE ETFs debut with impressive $54M trading volume, exceeding analysts' expectations.
data.description: New exchange-traded funds (ETFs) that track the performance of XRP and Dogecoin have exceeded expectations in terms of trading volume according to analysts. The two ETFs collectively recorded a total of $54 million in trades on their first day of trading.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin stable amid SEC's crypto ETF policy changes. BTC price at $117,273.58. BTC may rally to $130,000.
data.description: The leading cryptocurrencies were trading sideways, despite the Securities and Exchange Commission easing rules for institutional cryptocurrency products. At 9:18 p.m. ET, the prices were as follows: Bitcoin +0.58% at $117,273.58, Ethereum -0.06% at $4,603.96, XRP +0.05% at $3.08, Solana +0.76% at $247.71, and Dogecoin -0.41% at $0.2815. Bitcoin almost reached $118,000 before dropping to $116,527, with a 21% decrease in trading volumes. The SEC's approval of "generic listing standards" could make it easier for institutional capital to enter the market. Nearly $250 million was liquidated in the past 24 hours, with short liquidations totaling $160 million. Bitcoin's open interest increased by 2.40%. This, along with flat prices, suggests a potential large price move. Over 53% of top Bitcoin traders on Binance were active at the time.
